message:
✨ Add nightly Playwright QA workflow with e2e tests (#170)

- Add .github/workflows/nightly-qa.yml running at 6am UTC daily
- Install Playwright as dev dependency in web workspace
- Add playwright.config.ts for headless Chromium testing
- Add 7 e2e test files covering:
  - OSC 52 clipboard copy toast
  - Tile mode rendering (no blank tiles)
  - Tile selection persistence across reload
  - Active terminal/tab persistence across reload
  - Scroll containment within terminal viewport
  - Terminal scroll-to-bottom on mount
  - WebSocket reconnection resilience
- Workflow auto-creates GitHub issues with 'auto-qa' label on failure
- Exclude e2e/ from vitest to prevent test runner conflicts
- Add test:e2e and test:e2e:ui npm scripts

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,164 @@
+name: Nightly Playwright QA
+# Runs headless browser tests against copilot-remote nightly.
+# On failure, auto-opens a GitHub issue with label "auto-qa" and screenshot.
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # 6am UTC daily
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  issues: write
+
+env:
+  NODE_VERSION: '20'
+
+jobs:
+  playwright-tests:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 15
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Build server
+        run: npm run build --workspace=server
+
+      - name: Build web
+        run: npm run build --workspace=web
+
+      - name: Install Playwright browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
+        working-directory: web
+
+      - name: Start server in background
+        run: |
+          node server/dist/index.js &
+          SERVER_PID=$!
+          echo "SERVER_PID=$SERVER_PID" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
+          # Wait for server to be ready
+          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
+            if curl -sf http://localhost:3001/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+              echo "Server ready after ${i}s"
+              break
+            fi
+            sleep 1
+          done
+
+      - name: Run Playwright tests
+        id: playwright
+        run: npx playwright test --reporter=json 2>&1 | tee playwright-results.json || true
+        working-directory: web
+        env:
+          CI: true
+
+      - name: Check test results
+        id: check
+        run: |
+          cd web
+          # Parse the JSON report for failures
+          if [ -f playwright-results.json ]; then
+            FAILED=$(node -e "
+              const fs = require('fs');
+              try {
+                const r = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('playwright-results.json', 'utf8'));
+                const failed = (r.suites || []).flatMap(s =>
+                  (s.specs || []).filter(sp => sp.ok === false).map(sp => sp.title)
+                );
+                console.log(JSON.stringify(failed));
+              } catch { console.log('[]'); }
+            ")
+            echo "failed_tests=$FAILED" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            if [ "$FAILED" != "[]" ]; then
+              echo "has_failures=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            else
+              echo "has_failures=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            fi
+          else
+            echo "has_failures=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "failed_tests=[]" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Upload screenshots
+        if: steps.check.outputs.has_failures == 'true'
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: playwright-screenshots
+          path: web/test-results/
+          retention-days: 7
+
+      - name: Stop server
+        if: always()
+        run: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true
+
+      - name: Create issue on failure
+        if: steps.check.outputs.has_failures == 'true'
+        uses: actions/github-script@v7
+        with:
+          script: |
+            const failedTests = JSON.parse('${{ steps.check.outputs.failed_tests }}');
+            const runUrl = `${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`;
+            const date = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
+
+            const testList = failedTests.length > 0
+              ? failedTests.map(t => `- ❌ ${t}`).join('\n')
+              : '- ❌ (see workflow logs for details)';
+
+            const body = [
+              `## Nightly Playwright QA Failure — ${date}`,
+              '',
+              '### Failed Tests',
+              testList,
+              '',
+              '### Details',
+              `- **Workflow run**: ${runUrl}`,
+              `- **Branch**: \`${context.ref}\``,
+              `- **Commit**: \`${context.sha.slice(0, 8)}\``,
+              '',
+              '### Screenshots',
+              `Screenshots are attached as artifacts in the [workflow run](${runUrl}).`,
+              '',
+              '---',
+              '_Auto-generated by nightly-playwright-qa workflow._',
+            ].join('\n');
+
+            // Check for existing open issue to avoid duplicates
+            const { data: existing } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
+              owner: context.repo.owner,
+              repo: context.repo.repo,
+              labels: 'auto-qa',
+              state: 'open',
+              per_page: 5,
+            });
+
+            const duplicate = existing.find(i =>
+              i.title.includes('Nightly Playwright QA Failure')
+            );
+
+            if (duplicate) {
+              // Add a comment to the existing issue instead
+              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
+                owner: context.repo.owner,
+                repo: context.repo.repo,
+                issue_number: duplicate.number,
+                body: `### New failure detected — ${date}\n\n${testList}\n\n[Workflow run](${runUrl})`,
+              });
+              core.info(`Updated existing issue #${duplicate.number}`);
+            } else {
+              const { data: issue } = await github.rest.issues.create({
+                owner: context.repo.owner,
+                repo: context.repo.repo,
+                title: `🐛 Nightly Playwright QA Failure — ${date}`,
+                body,
+                labels: ['auto-qa'],
+              });
+              core.info(`Created issue #${issue.number}`);
+            }

@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';
+
+const PORT = 3001;
+
+export default defineConfig({
+  testDir: './e2e',
+  fullyParallel: false,
+  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,
+  retries: process.env.CI ? 1 : 0,
+  workers: 1,
+  reporter: process.env.CI ? 'github' : 'list',
+  timeout: 30_000,
+
+  use: {
+    baseURL: `http://localhost:${PORT}`,
+    screenshot: 'only-on-failure',
+    trace: 'retain-on-failure',
+  },
+
+  projects: [
+    {
+      name: 'chromium',
+      use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] },
+    },
+  ],
+});

@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@ export default defineConfig({
     environment: 'jsdom',
     globals: true,
     setupFiles: ['./src/test/setup.ts'],
+    exclude: ['e2e/**', 'node_modules/**'],
   },
   root: __dirname,
   plugins: [
